zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabel Lovell於1310年出生在英國諾薩普頓史爾拉普斯頓時，她的父親John Lovell 2nd Lord Lovel23歲，母親Maud Burnell20歲。她於大約1331年與WILLIAM DE CALTHORPE I在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾鐵其馬緒結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1349年之後，埋葬在英國英格蘭。

nameMeanings.name: Lovell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Anglo-Norman French lovel ‘wolf cub’, a diminutive of lou, love ‘wolf’. Compare Love and Low .

variants.name: Lowell
meanings: English: variant of Lovell , derived from Anglo-Norman Frenchlou ‘wolf’ + the diminutive suffix -el.

History: Lowell is the surname of one of America's most distinguished NewEngland families, which have been prominent for over 200 years. Itsfounder, John Lowell (1743–1802), was a legislator and judge. Thecity of Lowell, MA was named in honor of his son Francis Cabot Lowell(1775–1817), a textile manufacturer.

variants.name: Love
meanings: English: from a Middle English personal name derived from the Old English female name Lufu ‘love’, or the masculine equivalent Lufa. Compare Leaf 2.

English and Scottish: nickname from Anglo-Norman French love ‘she-wolf’ or simply ‘wolf’, the word love being a back-formation from the diminutive forms lovel and lovet ‘little wolf’. See also Low 3, from Anglo-Norman French lou, the more usual form of the word.
Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Fhionghuin (see McKinnon ).

variants.name: Loveall
meanings: English: presumably a variant of   Lovell  , or possibly a habitational name from Lovehall in Tayside.

variants.name: Lovewell
meanings: English (mainly Norfolk): from Middle English love ‘love’ (Old English lufian ‘to love’) + wel ‘well’ (Old English wel), presumably denoting a lover or philanderer. There may have been some confusion with Lovell .

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabel Lovell was born in 1310, in Thrapston, Northamptonshire, England, her father, John Lovell 2nd Lord Lovel, was 23 and her mother, Maud Burnell, was 20. She married WILLIAM DE CALTHORPE I about 1331, in Titchmarsh, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 1 daughter. She died after 1349, and was buried in England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabel Lovell est née en 1310, à Thrapston, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, son père, John Lovell 2nd Lord Lovel, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Maud Burnell, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé WILLIAM DE CALTHORPE I environ 1331, à Titchmarsh, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée après 1349, et a été enterrée à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Sie hat um 1331 in Titchmarsh, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom WILLIAM DE CALTHORPE I geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 8 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb nach 1349 und wurde in England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel Lovell è nata il nel 1310 nel Thrapston, Northamptonshire, England, sua padre, John Lovell 2nd Lord Lovel, aveva 23 anni e sua madre, Maud Burnell, aveva 20 anni. Lei sposato WILLIAM DE CALTHORPE I circa 1331, nel Titchmarsh, Northamptonshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 8 figli e 1 figlia. Morì dopo il 1349, e fu sepolta a Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Isabel Lovellが1310年にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアスラプストンで生まれた時，父John Lovell 2nd Lord Lovelは23歳，母Maud Burnellは20歳であった。約1331年にイギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアティトチマーッシュでWILLIAM DE CALTHORPE Iと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも8人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1349年以後に死去し，イギリスイギリスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Isabel Lovell(이)가 1310년 영국 노르섬프토실르 스라프스턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Lovell 2nd Lord Lovel(은)는 23세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Maud Burnell(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 대략 1331년 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 티트ch마시에서 WILLIAM DE CALTHORPE I(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1349년 이후 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com WILLIAM DE CALTHORPE I aproximadamente 1331, em Titchmarsh, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 8 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu após 1349, e foi sepultada em Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabel Lovell родилась в 1310 г. в местности трапстон, нортамптоншир, Англия, её отцу по имени John Lovell 2nd Lord Lovel было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Maud Burnell, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени WILLIAM DE CALTHORPE I около 1331 г. в местности тичмарш, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 1 дочь. Она умерла после 1349 г. и была похоронена в местности Англия, Великобритания.

Se casó con WILLIAM DE CALTHORPE I aproximadamente 1331, en Titchmarsh, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 8 hijos y 1 hija.
